items.title: Three fitting regimes on noisy data
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  data["Noisy training points"]
  uf["Underfit: too simple"]
  gf["Good fit: captures trend"]
  of["Overfit: wiggly through noise"]
  data --> uf
  data --> gf
  data --> of
items.caption: Underfitting misses structure; good fit tracks the signal; overfitting chases every training fluctuation.

items.title: Diagnosis from train vs validation error
items.mermaid:
flowchart TD
  start["Measure train and val error"]
  bothHigh["Both high?"]
  gap["Large train-val gap?"]
  under["Underfitting: add capacity or features"]
  over["Overfitting: regularize, more data, simplify"]
  good["Good fit: near minimum val error"]
  start --> bothHigh
  bothHigh -->|yes| under
  bothHigh -->|no| gap
  gap -->|yes| over
  gap -->|no| good
items.caption: High training and validation error together point to underfitting; a big gap with low train error points to overfitting.

items.title: Complexity moves along bias-variance curve
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  low["Low complexity"]
  mid["Moderate complexity"]
  high["High complexity"]
  uflabel["Underfitting zone"]
  sweet["Sweet spot"]
  oflabel["Overfitting zone"]
  low --> uflabel
  mid --> sweet
  high --> oflabel
items.caption: Increasing model complexity reduces underfitting then risks overfitting — validation error usually has a minimum in between.
